data.description: On April 29, 2026, in Pittsford, NY, homeowners and property owners are being advised on selecting a local paving expert for their driveway and pavement requirements. data.title: NJ Neurosurgeon First to Use Robotic-Assisted Technology in an Ambulatory Surgery Center
data.description: In April 29, 2026, Redefine Healthcare revealed that David Wells-Roth, MD, a board-certified and fellowship-trained neurosurgeon, achieved a significant milestone by becoming the inaugural provider globally to conduct robotic-assisted spine surgery in an ambulatory surgery center (ASC). This groundbreaking surgery marked a major advancement in medical technology and patient care, potentially altering the landscape of spine surgeries.

data.title: Fed Holds Rates Unchanged: Traders Brace For Powell's Likely Final Presser (UPDATED)
data.description: The Federal Reserve decided to keep the benchmark federal funds rate steady at 3.50-3.75% in the latest policy decision led by Chair Jerome Powell. There was a notable dissent from Governor Stephen Miran who favored a 0.25% rate cut. Concerns about elevated inflation due to surging global energy prices were highlighted, signaling a more hawkish stance compared to the previous assessment of inflation as "somewhat elevated." The Committee also emphasized the impact of developments in the Middle East on creating uncertainty in the economic outlook. Notably, members Beth M. Hammack, Neel Kashkari, and Lorie K. Logan supported maintaining rates without introducing any easing bias. This decision is perceived as Powell's final move before Kevin Warsh takes over as Chair on May 15.

data.title: Pfizer Says Cancer Therapy Beats Standard Therapy In Late-Stage Blood Cancer Study
data.description: Pfizer Inc. revealed results from a late-stage study of its cancer drug, ELREXFIO (elranatamab), targeting multiple myeloma, a type of blood cancer impacting plasma cells produced in the bone marrow. This cancer is the second most prevalent across the globe, with 36,000 new cases annually in the U.S. and 187,000 worldwide. Pfizer's Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial, examining ELREXFIO for multiple myeloma, reported a significant enhancement in the primary endpoint of progression-free survival. ELREXFIO is currently approved in more than 35 countries. This promising development comes as major drug companies consider Inhibrx cancer medications valued at over $8 billion, causing stock values to rise.
